title: BAI Implements 3x15 Scoring System for Domestic Tournaments
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India has introduced a new 3x15 scoring system for U13, U15, and U17 domestic ranking tournaments and National Championships, set to debut in Ernakulam.
summary: The Badminton Association of India (BAI) has announced the implementation of a new 3x15 scoring system for all domestic ranking tournaments and National Championships in the U-13, U-15, and U-17 categories, with its first use scheduled in Ernakulam.

title: Kidambi Srikanth Finishes Runner-Up at US Open
indian_angle: Indian shuttler Kidambi Srikanth reached the final of the US Open Super 300 event, showcasing his continued form on the international circuit.
summary: Kidambi Srikanth's strong run at the US Open ended in the final, where he was defeated by Su Li Yang. This marks a significant performance for the Indian shuttler in a Super 300 tournament.

title: Rounak Chouhan Stuns World No. 6 Chou Tien Chen at US Open
indian_angle: Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan achieved a significant upset by defeating World No. 6 Chou Tien Chen at the US Open Super 300 event.
summary: Young Indian shuttler Rounak Chouhan caused a stir at the US Open Super 300 by beating the much higher-ranked Chou Tien Chen, marking a notable victory in his career.

title: Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Tournament in Chennai Next Month
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India has announced the Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Badminton Tournament 2026 will be held in Chennai from July 16th to 23rd.
summary: Chennai will host the Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Badminton Tournament 2026 from July 16th to 23rd, as per a recent BAI circular, inviting top senior players from across the country.

title: Thomas Cup Squad Reassembles for Asian Games, Treesa-Gayatri Return
indian_angle: India's Thomas Cup winning squad is reassembling for the upcoming Asian Games, with the notable return of the women's doubles pair Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand.
summary: The Indian badminton squad that achieved historic success at the Thomas Cup is preparing for the Asian Games, featuring the return of key players like Treesa Jolly and Gayatri Gopichand to bolster the team.

title: India Invites Bids for BWF World Championships 2026 Organization
indian_angle: India is set to host the prestigious BWF World Championships in 2026, with the Badminton Association of India inviting Expressions of Interest for its organization.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has issued an Expression of Interest for the organization of the BWF World Championships 2026, confirming India's role as the host nation for the major international event.
